original: Scientists claim to have found dinosaur remains killed on the actual day a giant asteroid struck earth 66 million years ago beginning their extinction, at Tanis fossil site, North Dakota
First ever "Marsquake" seismic event on planet Mars detected by NASA's InSight lander
Post It Notes introduced
Teflon invented by American chemist Roy J. Plunkett at DuPont
Recording-breaking price of $3.25 million for a comic book as Action Comics #1, that introduced Superman for the first time, sells at auction
Nadia, a tiger at the Bronx Zoo (New York City), tests positive for COVID-19, 1st known case of human-to-cat transmission
First baby born with DNA from three parents through mitochondrial transfer in Mexico
Guinness world record for largest afro at 5ft 5in (165cm) set for the third time by Aevin Dugas of Reserve, Louisiana
1st time 11 people in space
America launches the Pioneer 11 (Pioneer G) probe to study Jupiter & Saturn
Harold Edgerton, American electrical engineer and photographer who created high-speed photography techniques, born in Fremont, Nebraska (d. 1990)
World's first animated cartoon is released, "Humorous Phases of Funny Faces" by J. Stuart Blackton
Soccer ball juggled non-stop for 14:14 hrs
Hostess Twinkies invented by bakery executive James Dewar
Intelsat 1 ("Early Bird") 1st commercial geosynchronous communications satellite launched
Rockwell B-1B Lancers break 11 world speed records
Karnataka beat Uttar Pradesh by inn & 193 to win Ranji Trophy

Washington Capitals left wingAlex Ovechkinbecomes the greatest scorer in NHL history passing Wayne Gretzky with his 895th career goal in a 4-1 loss to the NY Islanders at UBS Arena in Elmont, New York
Peyton List, American actress (Diary of a Wimpy Kid), born in Florida
Mingyu [Kim Min-gyu], South Korean singer and rapper (Seventeen), born in Anyang, South Korea

First modern Summer Olympic Games open in Athens, Greece; American athlete James Connolly becomes the first modern Olympic champion when he wins the triple jump (then 2 hops and a jump); later places 3rd in long jump and 2nd in high jump
North Pole almost reached by American explorersRobert Pearyand Matthew Henson. Navigational errors discovered decades later in the travel log likely place the expedition a few miles short.
George Eastmanbegins selling Kodak flexible roll film for the first time
Linus Pauling's seminal chemistry work "The Nature of the Chemical Bond" is published establishing valence bond theory
Philip Henry Gosse, English naturalist (created 1st public aquarium at London Zoo, coined term 'aquarium'), born in Worcester, England (d. 1888)
11th NASA Space Shuttle Mission (41C): Challenger 5 launches
Columbia returns to Kennedy Space Center from White Sands
New Zealand Sign Language is made an official language of New Zealand
New York Yankee Ron Blomberg becomes MLB's 1st designated hitter; he walks on five pitches from Boston Red Sox Luis Tiant at Fenway Park
Tampa Bay Lightning beats Boston Bruins, 6-3 in the season finale for their 62nd regular season win, tying the NHL record held by Detroit Red Wings (1995-96); 128 points, 4th in NHL history
H Levitt sinks 499 basketball free throws, misses & sinks 371 more
4 planes leave Seattle on 1st successful around-the-world flight
Paolo Nespoli, Italian engineer and astronaut who took part in space shuttle mission STS-120, born in Milan Italy
Empress of RussiaCatherine the Greatends the tax on men with beards, enacted by TsarPeter the Greatin 1698
1st transcontinental glider tow completed
African American polar explorer Matthew Henson buried next to Robert Peary in Arlington National Cemetery, in Arlington, Virginia
Virginia Hall [Goillot], American spy with British Special Operations during WWII (1940-66), born in Baltimore, Maryland (d. 1982)

William Dawes, American soldier and patriot who with Paul Revere and Samuel Prescott warned Massachusetts minutemen the regulars are coming before the battles of Lexington and Concord, born in Boston, Province of Massachusetts Bay, British America (d. 1799)[1]
American baseball playerAlbert Belleshows off his arm by hitting Sports Illustrated photographer Tony Tomsic in the hand prior to a game
Roberto ClementeDay, Pittsburgh Pirates retire his #21
Middleweight World Boxing Championship bout:Sugar Ray Leonardupsets Marvelous Marvin Hagler at Caesars Palace in Paradise, Nevada
Gordie Howecompletes a record 26th NHL season
19th Eurovision Song Contest:ABBAfor Sweden wins singing "Waterloo" in Brighton, England
Black Francis [Charles Thompson IV], American singer and songwriter (Pixies; Frank Black), born in Boston, Massachusetts
Gil Kane [Eli Katz], Latvian-American comic book artist known for his work on the Green Lantern comics, born in Riga, Latvia (d. 2000)
Sterling Sharpe, American NFL wide receiver (Green Bay Packers; 3x First-team All-Pro, 5x Pro Bowl, Pro Football Hall of Fame) and NFL Network analyst, born in Chicago, Illinois
Robert Earnshaw, Welsh soccer striker (59 caps; scored hat-tricks in Premier League, Divisions 1, 2 & 3, FA Cup, League Cup and international match; Cardiff City), born in Mufulira, Zambia
Lauren Ridloff, American actress (The Walking Dead; Eternals), born in Chicago, Illinois
Joy Oladokun, American folk-rock and R&B, rock singer-songwriter ("Who Do I Turn To?"; "I See America"), born in Casa Grande, Arizona
"Star Trek" film reboot directed byJ.J. Abrams, starring Chris Pine and Zachary Quinto premieres in Austin, Texas
Exhibition of 1960s iconMary Quantopens at London's V&A Museum

Benny Andersson &Björn Ulvaeus' musical "Mamma Mia!", featuring the songs ofABBA, opens at the Prince Edward Theatre in London's West End; transfers twice to other venues; run interrupted by COVID-19 in March of 2020
200,000 attend rock concert "California Jam" at the Ontario Motor Speedway in Ontario, California; line-up includes Earth, Wind & Fire; Black Sabbath; Deep Purple; and Emerson, Lake & Palmer
Judge rules Beatles 1962 Hamburg album can be released
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Dhaka: captain Meg Lanning's 44 from 30 balls leads Australia to 106/4; beat England 105/8 to win by 6 wickets (with 29 balls remaining); 3rd consecutive T20 WC
Subhana, becomes 1st Australian woman to become a Zen teacher
HemisFair 1968 opens in San Antonio, Texas
Scottish nobles reaffirm their independence by signing the Declaration of Arbroath addressed to Pope John XXII
Construction of the Red Fort completed at Shahjahanabad, Mughal Empire (now Dehli, India)
French explorer Louis-Antoine de Bougainville's expedition arrives in Tahiti, claiming it for France and naming it New Cythera
